title: Elasticity
summary: Price elasticity of demand, the midpoint formula, determinants of elasticity, relationship with total revenue, cross-price elasticity, income elasticity, and price elasticity of supply.
content_html:
Elasticity

Elasticity measures how much one economic variable responds to changes in another. It is essential for business pricing decisions.

Price Elasticity of Demand

Price elasticity of demand measures the responsiveness of quantity demanded to a change in price.

Formula (Midpoint Method):

PED = (Q2 - Q1) / ((Q1 + Q2) / 2)  divided by  (P2 - P1) / ((P1 + P2) / 2)

The midpoint formula ensures the same elasticity value whether price rises or falls.

Types of Demand Elasticity

  • Elastic demand: |PED| > 1 -- % change in Qd > % change in P. Consumers are price-sensitive.
  • Inelastic demand: |PED| < 1 -- % change in Qd < % change in P. Consumers are price-insensitive.
  • Unit-elastic: |PED| = 1 -- % changes are equal.
  • Perfectly inelastic: |PED| = 0 -- quantity demanded does not change with price (e.g. life-saving medication).
  • Perfectly elastic: |PED| = infinity -- any price increase causes quantity to drop to zero.

Determinants of Price Elasticity of Demand

  • Availability of substitutes (more substitutes = more elastic).
  • Whether the good is a necessity or luxury (necessities tend to be inelastic).
  • How narrowly the market is defined (broader = less elastic).
  • Passage of time (more elastic in long run -- consumers find alternatives).
  • Share of the good in the consumer's budget (larger share = more elastic).

Elasticity and Total Revenue

  • Elastic demand: Price and total revenue move in OPPOSITE directions.
  • Inelastic demand: Price and total revenue move in the SAME direction.
  • Unit-elastic: Total revenue does not change with price.

Other Elasticity Measures

  • Cross-price elasticity of demand: % change in Qd of Good A / % change in P of Good B. Positive = substitutes. Negative = complements.
  • Income elasticity of demand: % change in Qd / % change in income. Positive = normal good. Negative = inferior good.
  • Price elasticity of supply: % change in Qs / % change in P. Always positive. More elastic in the long run as producers have more time to adjust.

exercises.title: Elastic demand definition
options: The absolute value of PED is less than 1
The absolute value of PED equals 1
The absolute value of PED is greater than 1
Quantity demanded does not change with price
exercises.answer: The absolute value of PED is greater than 1

exercises.title: Midpoint formula purpose
exercises.prompt: The midpoint formula for calculating elasticity is used because:
options: It is simpler than the basic formula
It gives the same elasticity value whether price rises or falls between two points
It uses percentage changes in quantity only
It measures supply elasticity more accurately
exercises.answer: It gives the same elasticity value whether price rises or falls between two points

exercises.title: Elastic demand and revenue
exercises.prompt: If demand is elastic and a firm raises its price, total revenue will:

exercises.title: Long run elasticity
exercises.prompt: Price elasticity of demand is generally MORE elastic in the long run because:
options: Prices are higher in the long run
Consumers have more time to find alternatives and substitutes
Producers supply more in the long run
exercises.answer: Consumers have more time to find alternatives and substitutes
